systems.note: Lower bound — no organization, no retrieval support, no extraction. Useful only as a 'how fast can a list grow' baseline.
Framework hot-path cost. InMemorySTM enforces tier semantics + metadata stamping but skips any LLM work. The realistic floor for a Continuum deployment that uses STM-only retrieval.
error: TypeError("LLMEntityExtractor.extract() missing 1 required positional argument: 'gliner_entities'")
skipped: mem0 is installed but requires provider credentials (OpenAI / Anthropic) and a real LLM call per add(). Enable via MEM0_PROVIDER + MEM0_MODEL env vars and a follow-up PR.

narrative: Continuum's hot path (continuum_bare) costs 0.07ms/session at p50 vs 0.00ms for a raw list append — a 146.3x overhead to gain tier semantics, metadata indexing, and async-safe access. With production extraction enabled (continuum_full), the framework adds 0.0 LLM calls per 6-turn session — entirely on a background queue in real deployments — for a p50 of 0.00ms/session (LLM calls mocked; real wall-clock = mocked + N x your provider latency). The trade is paid once per turn and amortises across all subsequent retrievals.
